action.reasoning: Observation: Iter3 failed at SubprocVecEnv creation with ConnectionResetError (multiprocessing pipe issue, common with nested funcs or env in some runners); iter1 gave 0.0 success (20k steps too few); predict.py works but eval uses old weak checkpoint. Analysis: DummyVecEnv avoids multiprocessing entirely (Minigrid steps are fast, 8 envs sufficient); code structure (SB3 PPO + FlatObsWrapper + MlpPolicy + post-eval logging to metrics.jsonl) is correct per task; 500k timesteps should yield >0 success on easy envs like DoorKey/LavaGap. Decision: Rewrite train.py to use DummyVecEnv(8), wrap exec in if __name__=='__main__', set 500k steps + n_episodes=3 for speed, keep all else identical; run training then immediately submit_eval to get first non-zero baseline + measure iter time (~10-20min expected).
